text: Endurance running programs for physically inactive adults typically consist of 3.7 sessions/week, 2.3 hours/week, 14.4 km/week at 60-90% of maximum heart rate
After 1 year of running training, physically inactive adults can expect a body mass reduction of approximately 3.3 kg
After 1 year of running training, body fat percentage decreases by approximately 2.7% in previously inactive adults
Running training reduces resting heart rate by approximately 6.7 beats per minute after 1 year in previously inactive adults
Running training increases VO2max by approximately 7.1 ml/min/kg after 1 year in previously inactive adults
Running training reduces triglycerides by approximately 16.9 mg/dl after 1 year in previously inactive adults
Running training increases HDL cholesterol by approximately 3.3 mg/dl after 1 year in previously inactive adults
Longer duration of running training produces larger health benefit effect sizes, supporting a dose-response relationship between training length and health outcomes
